prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `b1986d4b`.
codex_response:
Implemented `b1986d4b` as an ARC2 task package in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1986d4b/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1986d4b/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1986d4b/generator.py#L175), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1986d4b/notes.md). The rule is a canonical 5-row nested-footprint summary of the `2x2`, `3x3`, and `4x4` square colors, with one correction to the hint: a bottommost `2x2` below the last `3x3` is a distractor and should be ignored. I used the `opus46` hint as a starting point, but corrected that mismatch in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1986d4b/notes.md); no new shared primitive was needed.

Verification is complete: official examples passed `4/4`, the shared build produced 1000 examples, `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task b1986d4b` passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked the generated previews. The generator explicitly places the three square sizes, optional extra bottom `2x2` decoys, and sparse singleton/domino noise so the inputs stay close to the original family.
